title: Operation Righteous Cowboy Lightning

jokes.text: it's still fun to look up and pretend all the buildings are giant severed robot penises
jokes.setup: Talking about the timeless appeal of looking up at NYC buildings
jokes.punchline: Comparing them to severed robot penises
jokes.explanation: Classic 30 Rock absurdist opener - takes mundane observation and adds bizarre, specific twist that's both shocking and oddly relatable

jokes.text: Be quiet, Lemon. It's happening.
jokes.setup: Liz just made an absurd comment about robot penises
jokes.punchline: Jack's dismissive response treating her weird observation as unimportant compared to the corporate takeover
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack - completely ignoring Liz's weirdness to focus on business priorities with dramatic gravity

jokes.text: It seems like one of us should sing The Circle Game right now
jokes.setup: Corporate transition happening, nostalgic moment
jokes.punchline: Self-aware comment about the clichéd appropriateness of the Joni Mitchell song
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about TV/movie clichés - typical Liz being aware of the moment's dramatic convention

jokes.text: I'm like Keats' 'Stout Cortez,' staring at the Pacific with a wild surmise and daring to imagine what... New planets might swim into my ken
jokes.setup: Jack describing his corporate transition philosophically
jokes.punchline: Overwrought literary reference that's both pretentious and misattributed (it's Keats writing about Balboa, not Cortez)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack pomposity - classical education used for corporate transition, with literary error for extra pretentiousness

jokes.text: I was holding in a 'snart,' and then right when she took the photo...
jokes.setup: Kenneth wants to retake his ID photo because something went wrong
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was holding in a 'snart' during the photo
jokes.explanation: Kenneth's innocent delivery of made-up bodily function word - classic character-specific humor

jokes.text: Don't you want to know what a 'snart' is? I can tell you now. It's 10:00. 10:00 in the morning.
jokes.setup: Audience expects definition of 'snart'
jokes.punchline: Kenneth deflects by saying it's now 10am, avoiding explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - builds up audience expectation for gross-out explanation then pivots to innocent time announcement

jokes.text: FYI, Tracy's phoning it in today. What else is new? No, mother, literally. Are you also staying at this hotel? Line.
jokes.setup: Dotcom says Tracy is phoning it in (being lazy)
jokes.punchline: Tracy is literally on the phone, asking for his line
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay on 'phoning it in' - literal interpretation of figurative expression with perfect Tracy chaos

jokes.text: throwing telephones at hotel employees, speaking to the U.N. about some messed-up crap in Africa, and I'm definitely getting a private island
jokes.setup: Tracy describing what A-list actor craziness entails
jokes.punchline: Escalating from throwing phones to casual dismissal of African issues to island ownership
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of celebrity bad behavior with Tracy's casual attitude toward serious issues

jokes.text: Nicholas Cage, Celine Dion, Charles Widmore
jokes.setup: List of crazy A-listers who own islands
jokes.punchline: Includes fictional Lost character Charles Widmore among real celebrities
jokes.explanation: Sneaky inclusion of fictional character in list of real people - rewards pop culture knowledge

jokes.text: I'm like Stout Cortez! He's my gardener. He's easily amazed.
jokes.setup: Tracy references Jack's earlier Cortez metaphor
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's talking about his gardener named Cortez who gets amazed easily
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that subverts Jack's pretentious reference - Tracy's literal-minded interpretation is perfect character comedy

jokes.text: I'm gonna get a sandwich and then eat it on the toilet
jokes.setup: Tracy saying he won't rehearse
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions eating on the toilet
jokes.explanation: Typical Tracy gross-out humor - character-appropriate but not particularly clever

jokes.text: when I look into a mirror, there's just a white haze
jokes.setup: Kenneth hoping he photographs well on TV
jokes.punchline: Implies he has no reflection or appears as white haze in mirrors
jokes.explanation: Adds to Kenneth's mysterious supernatural undertones - vampire/ghost implications delivered innocently

jokes.text: I couldn't put the memo in your mailbox, 'cause it's full of unread adoption materials
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining why he couldn't deliver a memo
jokes.punchline: Jack's mailbox is stuffed with adoption papers he's ignoring
jokes.explanation: Dark character detail about Jack's personal life delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Oh, God, Lutz, why? What? I don't want tush lines.
jokes.setup: Liz reacts with horror to something Lutz is doing
jokes.punchline: Lutz is adjusting his underwear to avoid visible lines
jokes.explanation: Standard Lutz gross-out moment - character-appropriate but predictable

jokes.text: Sullivan Psychiatric... You'll drool over our crazy prices
jokes.setup: Jack mentioning their product placement sponsor
jokes.punchline: Psychiatric clinic's tagline plays on 'drooling' as side effect
jokes.explanation: Dark pun on psychiatric medication side effects - typical 30 Rock corporate satire

jokes.text: the real transvestite hoarders of Orange County Penitentiary
jokes.setup: Jack listing terrible reality TV shows
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and absurd reality show concept
jokes.explanation: Escalating absurdity in reality TV show names - good media satire

jokes.text: Why does the warden let Lady Extravaganza have so many spoons?
jokes.setup: Liz commenting on the fake reality show
jokes.punchline: Treats the absurd show as real, worried about specific character
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz getting invested in fake show details - character-driven absurdity with great specificity

jokes.text: I stole a cab from a pregnant lady on crutches, and I am just waiting...
jokes.setup: Liz talking about bad karma
jokes.punchline: Escalating description of her morning's terrible behavior
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Liz's selfish behavior - character-appropriate moral flexibility

jokes.text: What about a tornado that hits a handgun factory, and it's just spinning around shooting handguns?
jokes.setup: Brainstorming natural disasters for the fake benefit
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and violent disaster scenario
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation - combines natural disaster with guns for maximum American chaos

jokes.text: What happens when the second flood comes, and you aren't allowed on reverend Gary's ark? Especially since reverend Gary made it clear the ark is just for teenage boys.
jokes.setup: Religious disaster scenario about Noah's ark
jokes.punchline: Reveals the reverend's ark is only for teenage boys (pedophile implication)
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about religious abuse - pushes boundaries but relies on shock value

jokes.text: There was a cyclone in Brooklyn last year. It destroyed two vintage-t-shirt stores and a banjo.
jokes.setup: Talking about real weather disasters in NYC
jokes.punchline: Hipster-specific destruction (vintage shirts and banjo)
jokes.explanation: Good social satire about Brooklyn gentrification - specific cultural references work well

jokes.text: right before the ark leaves, I'm supposed to castrate reverend Gary
jokes.setup: Continuing the emergency plan discussion
jokes.punchline: Callback to reverend Gary with violent justice
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier joke with vigilante justice - doesn't add much to the original

jokes.text: I'm just continuing my consistent professional behavior. Let's laugh together, friend.
jokes.setup: Tracy acting unusually professional
jokes.punchline: Robotic, artificial friendliness that's clearly fake
jokes.explanation: Good character reversal - Tracy being fake-professional is inherently funny

jokes.text: perfection is my middle name... 'Unclaimed Perfection Baby Boy'
jokes.setup: Tracy saying perfection is his middle name
jokes.punchline: His actual middle name is 'Unclaimed Perfection Baby Boy'
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection revealing absurd backstory - classic Tracy family dysfunction

jokes.text: I'm good at archery and kind of want to die
jokes.setup: Listing skills for post-apocalyptic survival
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions suicidal thoughts alongside archery
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly - good juxtaposition of skills and depression

jokes.text: Toofer can get us through black, gay, and nerd-controlled neighborhoods
jokes.setup: Listing Toofer's apocalypse skills
jokes.punchline: His multiple identity categories as survival advantages
jokes.explanation: Character-specific humor about identity intersections - works because it's true to Toofer

jokes.text: Cerie will be some sort of queen in the new society
jokes.setup: Describing post-apocalyptic roles
jokes.punchline: Beautiful Cerie would naturally become royalty
jokes.explanation: Character observation about beauty privilege - true to Cerie but predictable

jokes.text: I will do sex with cannibals as needed
jokes.setup: Listing survival skills
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact about prostitution with cannibals
jokes.explanation: Dark shock humor - relies more on taboo than cleverness

jokes.text: I can talk to animals. Well, not talk to 'em... I can take commands from 'em
jokes.setup: Claims ability to talk to animals
jokes.punchline: Clarifies he can only take orders from them
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - subverts expectation by making the power dynamic backwards

jokes.text: It's like a reality show where you guys get to compete for a seat in my car by proving how much you like me
jokes.setup: Lutz having to choose who gets in his car
jokes.punchline: Compares it to a reality dating/competition show
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about reality TV format applied to mundane situation - fits Lutz's media awareness

jokes.text: No, I wasn't gonna buy two blimps and crash them into each other to see what sound they made
jokes.setup: Liz asking if Tracy had plans
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and expensive plan involving blimp destruction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy absurdity - wealthy enough and curious enough to crash blimps for science

jokes.text: I'm as happy as a clam that wants to kill some woman
jokes.setup: Tracy saying he's happy using clam metaphor
jokes.punchline: The clam is homicidal toward women
jokes.explanation: Subverts innocent idiom with dark twist - typical Tracy logic disruption

jokes.text: But I'm so identified with New York, you bloody tosser!
jokes.setup: Bob protesting he's identified with New York
jokes.punchline: Uses British insult while claiming New York identity
jokes.explanation: Good irony - character contradicts his claim with his language choice

jokes.text: But we're not laughing now, because our laughter excites the birds sexually
jokes.setup: Describing bird attack disaster scenario
jokes.punchline: Reveals bizarre reason they can't laugh at birds
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd escalation - takes Hitchcock reference to completely unexpected sexual territory

jokes.text: This wonderful flood that put out that devastating wildfire
jokes.setup: Reading disaster benefit scripts
jokes.punchline: Flood is 'wonderful' because it solved the fire problem
jokes.explanation: Good ironic framing - disaster as positive because it solved another disaster

jokes.text: ♪ Shut your mouth ♪ ♪ I am finished taking orders from you ♪ ♪ and I think that you're a four-eyed douche ♪
jokes.setup: Tracy singing to Billy Joel tune
jokes.punchline: Insulting Liz to recognizable melody
jokes.explanation: Character-driven musical moment - Tracy's childish rebellion through song

jokes.text: ♪ and also let me say that Liz is a ho ♪ ♪ a dirty ho ♪
jokes.setup: Continuing the insulting song
jokes.punchline: Escalates to calling Liz a 'dirty ho'
jokes.explanation: Predictable escalation - relies on shock value more than cleverness

jokes.text: ♪ I'm not good at making up songs ♪ ♪ unlike me, who is good ♪ ♪ as you can tell from this rhyme ♪
jokes.setup: Song battle between Tracy and Liz
jokes.punchline: Tracy contradicts himself within the same verse
jokes.explanation: Self-referential contradiction - Tracy's logic problems demonstrated in real time

jokes.text: ♪ help the people ♪ ♪ the thing that happened ♪ ♪ happened to ♪
jokes.setup: Generic disaster benefit song
jokes.punchline: Vague, fill-in-the-blank lyrics for any disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect satire of vague charity songs - generic enough for any disaster

jokes.text: Fantastic, Jenna. You really brought the songwriting computer's words to life
jokes.setup: Jack praising Jenna's performance
jokes.punchline: Reveals the song was written by computer
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan revelation about corporate efficiency - Jack's soulless approach to art

jokes.text: One of us is an actor, and actors are not people
jokes.setup: Liz explaining why she can't have adult conversation with Tracy
jokes.punchline: Declares actors aren't people
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about Hollywood - perfect Liz cynicism with universal truth

jokes.text: Someone get a P.A. to feed me baby food, or I will drop a 'd' in the green room!
jokes.setup: Jenna making demands
jokes.punchline: Threatens to defecate in green room if not fed baby food
jokes.explanation: Peak Jenna diva behavior - absurd demands with gross threats

jokes.text: Mmm! I don't really have a car
jokes.setup: Lutz has been the driver in their escape plan
jokes.punchline: Reveals he doesn't actually own a car
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lutz moment - undermines entire plan with casual admission

jokes.text: That was me singing the Beatles' Here Comes the Sun for free.
jokes.setup: Liz using auto-tune to remove melody
jokes.punchline: Tracy claiming Beatles song without copyright issues
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of copyright battle theme

jokes.text: Yeah, Tupac is alive. I bet you'd like to have that on your show
jokes.setup: Tracy mentioning he's going to Tupac's house
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals Tupac is alive
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy conspiracy theory delivered casually - perfect character moment

jokes.text: Five years ago, I rescued your career. And how do you repay me? By making my life harder at every turn
jokes.setup: Liz finally confronting Tracy
jokes.punchline: Lists her grievances with dramatic intensity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment but more dramatic than comedic - sets up the conflict

jokes.text: And your online romance prank was not funny! I fell in love with you!
jokes.setup: Liz listing Tracy's offenses
jokes.punchline: Reveals she actually fell in love during his prank
jokes.explanation: Good callback revealing unexpected emotional consequences of Tracy's pranks

jokes.text: I rode in here on a white horse that you made me leave in the lobby
jokes.setup: Tracy describing how he saved the show
jokes.punchline: Literally rode a white horse that had to be parked
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy absurdity - literal interpretation of metaphor with practical consequences

jokes.text: This honky grandma be trippin' over surfmaster's mid-range jet skis!
jokes.setup: Showing alternate reality without Tracy
jokes.punchline: Liz speaking in exaggerated urban slang about jet skis
jokes.explanation: Good alternate reality joke - shows how Liz would be worse without Tracy's influence

jokes.text: But then I would run you over with a jet ski! Damn it!
jokes.setup: Liz in alternate reality argument
jokes.punchline: Violent threat with inappropriate vehicle
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of alternate Liz violence, callback to jet skis

jokes.text: May Durga's trident pierce them from the skies!
jokes.setup: Jonathan asking if disaster was in Indian Kashmir
jokes.punchline: Expresses violent Hindu curse
jokes.explanation: Jonathan's mysterious background revealed through religious violence - good character detail

jokes.text: maybe a pelican near some diapers
jokes.setup: Jack describing disaster footage they'll get
jokes.punchline: Random specific image of pelican near diapers
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific disaster imagery - good absurd detail but not exceptional

jokes.text: Heavy structural damage, no fatalities... Sad, but not too sad.
jokes.setup: Jack describing perfect disaster
jokes.punchline: Calculating emotional response for ratings
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jack corporate cynicism, perfect disaster calculus

jokes.text: maybe a pelican near some diapers
jokes.setup: Jack describing ideal disaster footage
jokes.punchline: Specific random sympathetic image
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific absurd detail for disaster porn

jokes.text: Operation Righteous Cowboy Lightning is a go
jokes.setup: Jack naming his disaster benefit operation
jokes.punchline: Absurdly patriotic and dramatic code name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack grandiosity - overwrought military-style naming for TV scheme

jokes.text: You 'snarted' in my dressing room?
jokes.setup: Reference to Kenneth's earlier 'snart'
jokes.punchline: Tracy's horrified reaction to the unknown bodily function
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier joke but doesn't add much new comedy value

jokes.text: Operation Righteous Cowboy Lightning is a go.
jokes.setup: Jack activating his plan
jokes.punchline: Using the ridiculous operation name
jokes.explanation: Good callback to absurd operation name, Jack's grandiosity

jokes.text: If I hugged you, I would angle it so that you got no boob. And I would anticipate your angling, and I would get there. I would get there.
jokes.setup: Discussing fake reconciliation hug
jokes.punchline: Strategic planning for boob contact during hug
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of competitive strategic thinking applied to inappropriate hug geometry

jokes.text: the Fijian island owned by controversial actor Mel Gibson
jokes.setup: News report about the disaster location
jokes.punchline: Island belongs to controversial Mel Gibson
jokes.explanation: Topical reference to Gibson's controversies - makes the disaster less sympathetic

jokes.text: No word yet on the fate of Gibson's collection of anti-semitic and misogynistic literature
jokes.setup: Continuing news report about Gibson's property
jokes.punchline: References his offensive book collection
jokes.explanation: Escalation of Gibson satire - specific reference to his real controversies

jokes.text: G'day. First off, the holocaust never happened. Aah!
jokes.setup: Gibson family member being interviewed
jokes.punchline: Immediately expresses Holocaust denial
jokes.explanation: Dark shock humor referencing Gibson family controversies - relies on real-world knowledge

jokes.text: specifically Mel Gibson and his houseguest, Jon Gosselin
jokes.setup: Benefit for disaster victims
jokes.punchline: Victims are controversial celebrities
jokes.explanation: Absurd pairing of controversial figures as disaster victims - makes charity questionable

jokes.text: Will go towards rebuilding... His sex jacuzzi
jokes.setup: Money will help rebuild
jokes.punchline: Specifically Gibson's 'sex jacuzzi'
jokes.explanation: Inappropriate charity target - makes the benefit obviously problematic

jokes.text: There's a dealer sticker in the window, you got no plates, and you made these flames out of magic marker and tape!
jokes.setup: Lutz claiming to have owned car for a while
jokes.punchline: Evidence he just bought/made the car
jokes.explanation: Good detective work exposing Lutz's deception - specific visual evidence

jokes.text: It's all like it was before!
jokes.setup: Lutz being exposed as fraud
jokes.punchline: Desperate attempt to maintain status quo
jokes.explanation: Good Lutz desperation, pathetic attempt to recover

jokes.text: It's all like it was before!
jokes.setup: Lutz trying to maintain normalcy after being exposed
jokes.punchline: Desperate insistence that nothing changed
jokes.explanation: Pathetic Lutz moment - trying to maintain social standing after exposure

jokes.text: I knew I'd get that boob squish
jokes.setup: Tracy and Liz hug for cameras
jokes.punchline: Tracy successfully achieved his strategic hug goal
jokes.explanation: Callback to strategic hug planning - Tracy wins the contest

jokes.text: when the story doesn't have an ending you don't just create one out of thin air by playing music or having people give each other meaningful looks
jokes.setup: Liz criticizing reality TV manipulation
jokes.punchline: Describes exactly what sitcoms do for emotional moments
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor - Liz criticizing TV manipulation while show prepares to do exactly that

jokes.text: You know, some people actually craft stories. And when the story doesn't have an ending you don't just create one out of thin air by playing music or having people give each other meaningful looks.
jokes.setup: Liz criticizing reality TV
jokes.punchline: Describing exactly what's happening in the scene
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta joke - criticizing reality TV manipulation while doing exactly that

jokes.text: ♪ I'm gonna give all my secrets away ♪
jokes.setup: Liz just criticized manufactured emotional moments
jokes.punchline: Show immediately uses emotional music for manufactured moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta punchline - show does exactly what Liz just criticized, proving her point

tmdb_overview: A reality TV series trails Tracy, which Liz tries to exploit for her own gain. Jack does his own exploiting: He attempts to make a fast buck off natural-disaster telethons. And Lutz sets out to increase his worth in the event of an apocalypse.
insight: Military-chaos absurdism sustains 70 jokes across 41 minutes despite middling impact ratings.
